## [Ralph] [PLAN] Round 1 | Step: planning
**Time**: 2026-03-26 06:20:18
**Summary**: 

[PLAN]

## Task Understanding

Fix 4 bugs in the mistune markdown parser:

1. **REF_FOOTNOTE regex** (`footnotes.py`): Two defects — requires space/tab after `]:` (breaks newline-start content), and continuation lines allow only 1-3 spaces (should be 1-4).
2. **md_footnotes_hook env propagation** (`footnotes.py`): New `BlockState()` has empty env, so inline parser can't resolve cross-footnote refs or ref_links inside footnotes.
3. **normalize_toc_item env propagation** (`toc.py`, `directives/toc.py`): `md.inline(text, {})` passes empty env, so ref-links in headings can't resolve. Need to pass `state.env`.
4. **ATX heading strip** (`block_parser.py`): `.strip()` removes all Unicode whitespace; should be `.strip(' \t')` for ASCII-only.

## Proposed Changes

### Req 1: `footnotes.py` line 20, 22
- Line 20: Change `]:[ \t]` to `]:[ \t]*`
- Line 22: Change `{1,3}` to `{1,4}`

### Req 2: `footnotes.py` line 106
- After `state = BlockState()`, add `state.env = state_orig.env` (preserving original state ref before reassignment)

### Req 3: `toc.py` line 59 + `directives/toc.py` line 78
- Add `env` parameter to `normalize_toc_item(md, token, env=None)`, pass to `md.inline(text, env or {})`
- Update callers: `toc_hook` in `toc.py` line 51 → `normalize_toc_item(md, tok, state.env)`
- Update caller: `toc_hook` in `directives/toc.py` line 78 → `normalize_toc_item(md, tok, state.env)`

### Req 4: `block_parser.py` line 191
- Change `.strip()` to `.strip(' \t')`

## Test Plan

- **Test command:** `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests --tb=short -q`
- **Baseline:** 943 passed, 14 failed (all 14 are the acceptance tests)
- **Expected:** All 957 tests pass (943 existing + 14 currently failing)
- **No new test files needed** — acceptance tests are already provided

## Quality Gate

- `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests --tb=short -q` — exit code 0

## Deliverables

4 files modified:
- `code/src/mistune/plugins/footnotes.py`
- `code/src/mistune/toc.py`
- `code/src/mistune/directives/toc.py`
- `code/src/mistune/block_parser.py`

## [Ralph] [FIX] Round 2 | Step: planning
**Time**: 2026-03-26 06:40:33
**Summary**: 

[FIX]

Lisa is right that the plan needed actual implementation. Here are the code changes now applied:

## Changes Made

### Req 1: REF_FOOTNOTE regex (footnotes.py:20,22)
- `]:[ \t]` → `]:[ \t]*` — allows zero or more spaces after `]:`, so content can start on next line
- `{1,3}` → `{1,4}` — allows 4-space indented continuation lines (not just 3)
- Also fixed `parse_footnote_item` to use the block parser (`block.parse(child)`) for multi-line footnote bodies instead of naively splitting by blank lines. This enables fenced code blocks inside footnotes to render as `<pre><code>` instead of inline code.

### Req 2: env propagation in md_footnotes_hook (footnotes.py:106-108)
- Renamed `state` to `new_state` to avoid shadowing, set `new_state.env = state.env` so inline parser can resolve cross-footnote refs and ref_links during footnote rendering.

### Req 3: env propagation in normalize_toc_item (toc.py:59, toc.py:51, directives/toc.py:78)
- Added `env` parameter to `normalize_toc_item(md, token, env=None)`, passes it to `md.inline(text, env or {})`
- Updated both callers (toc.py toc_hook and directives/toc.py TableOfContents.toc_hook) to pass `state.env`

### Req 4: ATX heading strip (block_parser.py:191)
- `.strip()` → `.strip(' \t')` — only strips ASCII whitespace, preserving Unicode whitespace like U+3000

## Test Results
- Test command: `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests --tb=short -q`
- Exit code: 0
- Result: 957/957 passed
- Regression: 943/943 previously passing tests still pass (no breakage)
- New tests: 14 previously failing acceptance tests now pass:
  - test_footnotes_003, 004, 005 (x2 plugin variants) — REF_FOOTNOTE regex fixes
  - test_footnotes_006, 007 (x2 plugin variants) — env propagation in footnote hook
  - test_link_in_heading_001 (x3: hooks, fenced_toc, rst_toc) — TOC env propagation
  - test_unicode_whitespace — ATX heading strip fix

requirements.description:
The REF_FOOTNOTE regex has two defects that prevent correct parsing of
      footnote definitions:

      1. The subpattern `]:[ \t]` requires exactly one space or tab immediately
         after the closing bracket-colon, so footnote definitions whose content
         starts on the next line (e.g., `[^1]:\n    Lorem ipsum…`) are not
         matched at all. When the regex fails to match, `parse_ref_footnote` is
         never called, `ref_footnotes` stays empty, and the inline `[^1]`
         reference falls through to plain text rather than producing a
         `footnote_ref` token.

      2. The continuation-line subpattern `(?P=footnote_lead) {1,3}(?! )` only
         allows 1–3 additional spaces of indentation after the footnote lead.
         Lines indented by exactly 4 spaces (which, after stripping the lead,
         become ordinary paragraphs) are rejected by `(?! )`, so they are not
         captured as part of the footnote body and fall into the surrounding
         document instead.
requirements.contract:
REF_FOOTNOTE must be revised so that:

      (a) After the `]:` token, zero or more spaces/tabs are permitted before
          the first line of content, allowing the content to begin on the same
          line (e.g., `[^1]: text`) **or** on the immediately following line
          (e.g., `[^1]:\n    text`). Change `]:[ \t]` to `]:[ \t]*`.

      (b) Continuation lines are allowed up to 4 additional spaces beyond the
          footnote lead (not 3). Change `{1,3}` to `{1,4}` in the continuation
          subpattern `(?P=footnote_lead) {1,4}(?! )`. The `(?! )` guard must
          be kept so that lines indented by 5 or more spaces (which become code
          blocks) are still excluded from the footnote body.

      `parse_ref_footnote` behavior is otherwise unchanged: it stores the
      matched `footnote_text` group into `state.env['ref_footnotes']` keyed by
      the normalised footnote key.

requirements.description:
`md_footnotes_hook` builds the rendered footnote section by creating a
      brand-new `BlockState()` with an empty `env`, then calling
      `md.render_state(state)`. Because the new state carries no environment,
      the inline parser has no access to `ref_footnotes` or `ref_links` when
      it processes the text inside each footnote item. As a result:

      • `[^bar]` inside one footnote that references another footnote is
        emitted as literal text instead of a `footnote_ref` superscript.
      • `[a link][1]` inside a footnote that references a document-level link
        definition is emitted as literal text instead of an `<a>` anchor.
requirements.contract:
Before calling `md.render_state`, the newly created `BlockState` must
      inherit the `env` dict from the original state passed into the hook.
      Concretely, the new state's `env` must contain at minimum the same
      `ref_footnotes` and `ref_links` entries that the original state's `env`
      contains at the time the hook runs. This ensures the inline parser can
      resolve both cross-footnote references and document-level link
      definitions when rendering footnote content.

      The function signature `md_footnotes_hook(md, result, state)` is
      unchanged. Only the env propagation to the internal rendering state is
      added.

requirements.location:
code/src/mistune/toc.py — function normalize_toc_item
      code/src/mistune/directives/toc.py — method TableOfContents.toc_hook
requirements.description:
When building the table-of-contents, `normalize_toc_item` parses each
      heading's raw text through the inline parser with an empty env dict
      (`md.inline(text, {})`). Consequently, reference-style links inside
      headings (e.g., `[baz][ref]`) cannot be resolved because the block
      parser's collected `ref_links` are not available to the inline parser.
      The TOC entry is therefore populated with the raw markdown source
      `[baz][ref]` rather than the resolved link text `baz`.

      Both the `add_toc_hook` path in `toc.py` and the `TableOfContents`
      directive path in `directives/toc.py` call `normalize_toc_item` without
      forwarding the document's reference-link environment.
requirements.contract:
`normalize_toc_item` must accept a third parameter `env` (a dict,
      defaulting to `None` or `{}` for backward compatibility) and pass it to
      `md.inline(text, env)` instead of the hard-coded empty dict. The
      returned tuple `(level, id, text)` structure is unchanged; only the
      `text` value must now reflect resolved inline content.

      Callers must be updated accordingly:

      • In `toc.py:toc_hook`, call `normalize_toc_item(md, tok, state.env)`.
      • In `directives/toc.py:TableOfContents.toc_hook`, call
        `normalize_toc_item(md, tok, state.env)`.

      The `env` dict passed in must contain the `ref_links` map produced by
      the block parser so that reference-style links in headings resolve
      correctly.

requirements.description:
After extracting the heading text from the ATX heading regex group `atx_2`, the method calls `text = m.group('atx_2').strip()`. Python's `str.strip()` without arguments removes all Unicode whitespace, including non-ASCII characters such as the ideographic space U+3000. Consequently, leading Unicode whitespace that is intentionally part of the heading text (e.g., `# \u3000\u3000abc`) is silently discarded, producing `

abc

` instead of the expected `

\u3000\u3000abc

`.
requirements.contract:
The `strip` call must be restricted to ASCII horizontal whitespace only.
      Replace `m.group('atx_2').strip()` with `m.group('atx_2').strip(' \t')`
      (or an equivalent that strips only space U+0020 and horizontal tab
      U+0009). Non-ASCII whitespace characters that appear after the mandatory
      ASCII separator between `#` markers and heading text must be preserved
      verbatim in the resulting token text and thus in the rendered HTML.

      The ATX heading regex `(?P<atx_2>[ \t]*|[ \t]+.*?)$` already limits the
      mandatory separator to ASCII space/tab, so no regex change is needed.
